meta_title: For Students: deployment workflow to Solve no version control (Advanced Checklist)
meta_description: Learn how to deployment workflow and overcome no version control. A Advanced-level Checklist using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: Deployment Workflow: Fix No Version Control (Student Guide)
content.seo_description: Stop losing code. Learn a professional deployment workflow to solve version control issues using Antigravity and Claude Code in this advanced student guide.
content.answer_block: Without version control, you are one accidental delete away from total project loss. The solution is implementing a Git-based deployment pipeline that automates your commits and syncs your environment. By leveraging Antigravity for architectural oversight and Claude Code for CLI automation, you transform your workflow into a production-ready system.
content.intro:
You’ve spent weeks coding your first real-world SaaS, only to realize your project folder is a graveyard of 'final_v2_fixed' subdirectories. The fear of breaking your app has paralyzed your progress, leaving your best work vulnerable to a single misstep. This isn't just a lack of organization; it's a critical technical bottleneck that separates hobbyists from professional software engineers.

But relief is here. By integrating a disciplined deployment workflow, you move from manual file-copying to a robust, automated ecosystem. Using modern tools like Antigravity and Claude Code, you will build a safety net that protects your code, tracks every iteration, and prepares you for the high-velocity environment of professional engineering.

steps.title: Initialize Git with Strategic Ignoring
steps.description: Establish a clean repository foundation. You must ensure environment variables and build artifacts are never tracked, preventing security leaks.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a robust .gitignore file tailored for a full-stack AI SaaS project, ensuring node_modules, .env files, and dist folders are excluded.

steps.title: Configure Automated Commit Hooks
steps.description: Automate the 'why' of your commits. Using hooks ensures your code is linted and tested before it ever reaches the repository history.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Set up a pre-commit hook that runs ESLint and Prettier to ensure code quality before allowing a commit to proceed.
steps.claude_code_command: npx husky install && npx husky add .husky/pre-commit 'npm run lint'

steps.title: Establish the Deployment Branching Strategy
steps.description: Adopt a 'main' and 'develop' branch workflow. This protects your production environment while allowing you to experiment freely in feature branches.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Explain the mechanics of a GitFlow branching strategy for a student project and provide the CLI commands to switch between environments.

steps.title: Integrate Claude Code for CLI Assisted Commits
steps.description: Use Claude Code to generate meaningful commit messages based on your actual code changes, ensuring your history is readable for future employers.
steps.antigravity_prompt: How can I prompt Claude Code to analyze my current diff and write a conventional commit message following the semantic versioning standard?
steps.claude_code_command: claude commit --message 'feat: implement user authentication flow'

steps.title: Deploy via Automated CI/CD Pipeline
steps.description: Link your repository to a cloud provider like Vercel or Railway. Every push to 'main' should now trigger a live deployment.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a YAML configuration for a GitHub Actions workflow that automatically builds and deploys my app on push to the main branch.

pro_tips: Always use Conventional Commits (feat:, fix:, chore:) to make your GitHub contribution graph look professional to recruiters.
Never commit your .env file; use environment variable templates (.env.example) to share required configuration keys.
Use Claude Code to perform 'git diff' analysis before pushing; it catches logic errors you might have missed during late-night coding.
Treat your README.md as a product document; use it to explain the architecture, not just installation steps.
Implement a 'dry-run' deployment script to test your production build locally before it hits the cloud.
content.next_steps: After completing this workflow, begin integrating automated unit testing into your CI pipeline to ensure your app remains stable as it grows.
content.conversion_block: You now possess the professional deployment infrastructure used by senior engineers to ship complex AI applications. Don't let your code sit idle on your local machine—get it into the wild, iterate, and build your portfolio. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
